title: Ram Temple trust to hold key meet 5 days early, to decide Champat Rai's fate on July 6
content: The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust has advanced its meeting to July 6, where the resignations of General Secretary Champat Rai and member Anil Mishra are expected to be finalized. The Trust may also discuss operational restructuring for enhanced transparency and consider new members. Discussions will also cover improving visitor facilities, especially during extreme heat, and crowd management strategies.

title: ‘I hate men’ post in Ketan death case gets dentist suspended for 5 years
content: A Madhya Pradesh dental association has suspended its treasurer, Dr. Muskan Soni, for five years after she allegedly justified the murder of a Pune businessman on social media. The association cited "acts of indiscipline" and "highly inappropriate, offensive and disrespectful remarks" as reasons for the severe action. Despite an apology, the organization deemed it insufficient, emphasizing a zero-tolerance policy for conduct undermining ethical standards and professional dignity.

title: Anti-drone arsenal, radars & anti-aircraft guns: How Army is keeping Amarnath pilgrims safe
content: As the Amarnath yatra approaches, the Army has implemented a state-of-the-art security system featuring advanced drone detection technology and anti-aircraft weaponry to address aerial threats. Collaborating with various security agencies, this holistic strategy guarantees ongoing monitoring along pilgrimage routes. This comprehensive security arrangement is designed to protect the thousands of devotees participating in this important annual Hindu event.

title: 'I'm very happy': Jadeja reacts to Ben Stokes' shock retirement
content: Former India captain Ajay Jadeja lauded Ben Stokes' retirement, calling it the "right call" and a testament to his refusal to be dictated by external forces. Jadeja admired Stokes' principled stand, suggesting the all-rounder wouldn't tolerate administrators controlling his personal life.  Meanwhile, Sanjay Manjrekar expressed sadness, calling Stokes' departure an "end of an era" and highlighting his match-winning prowess on the big stage.

title: Hormuz, Suez, Taiwan Strait: Why the world’s supply chains now need war-room planning
content: Global supply chains face unprecedented threats as maritime chokepoints become geopolitical battlegrounds. The US-Iran conflict highlighted how disruptions in vital waterways like the Strait of Hormuz and Bab el-Mandeb trigger widespread economic fallout, from soaring freight rates to increased inflation. Other critical routes, including the Suez, Taiwan, Malacca, and Panama Canals, are also under scrutiny, demanding a shift towards war-room level planning for future crises.

title: Venture capitalist Marc Andreessen: ‘ChatGPT is a better doctor than 99% of doctors’
content: Venture capitalist Marc Andreessen boldly claims ChatGPT surpasses 99% of doctors, citing AI's superior data processing capabilities. He envisions AI as a vital tool for physicians, offering instant access to vast medical research and aiding in complex medication management. While acknowledging potential policy hurdles, Andreessen believes AI's practical advantages will drive its widespread adoption in healthcare, despite some studies highlighting AI's limitations in critical medical scenarios.

title: BSNL failed to restore landline; consumer commission orders Rs 15,000 compensation
content: A consumer's fight against BSNL has been vindicated by the Madhya Pradesh State Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission. The commission upheld an order for BSNL to restore a landline connection and pay Rs 15,000 in compensation. BSNL failed to provide evidence for its claims of cable damage and inability to repair, leading the commission to dismiss their appeal as baseless.

title: 4-year-old boy falls into 220-foot-deep borewell in Haryana; rescue operation under way
content: A four-year-old boy, Nirbhay, tragically fell into a 220-foot-deep borewell in Ambala's Dhanaura village while playing near his father.  Rescue operations involving NDRF, SDRF, and the Army are underway. The incident occurred around 6:30 am when the child was reportedly throwing soil into the open borewell and slipped.  Authorities were alerted after initial rescue attempts by family and locals failed.

title: 'We didn't hold him hostage': Punjab Kings' Shashank Singh breaks silence after FIR against him, father
content: Cricketer Shashank Singh has responded to an FIR alleging assault and illegal confinement by a domestic cook. Singh claims the cook was hired under false pretenses, was taking private photos, and his mother deleted them due to privacy concerns. He denies any physical altercation or holding the cook hostage, stating they considered calling the police themselves after discovering the alleged unauthorized photography.

title: '994 days of genocide': Microsoft engineer quits, emails thousands on his final day
content: A Microsoft data center technician in Italy resigned on his final day, emailing thousands of colleagues across Europe to accuse the company of powering Israel's war in Gaza through its Azure cloud and European data centers. Published by No Azure for Apartheid, the email cites the Unit 8200 surveillance exposé and urges Microsoft workers to "reclaim our labor" and divest.

title: Panic in central Delhi as massive fire breaks out at Barakhamba Road restaurant
content: On Tuesday afternoon, a fire incident occurred at the Parikrama Restaurant on Barakhamba Road in central Delhi, prompting an urgent reaction from emergency crews. The Delhi Fire Service effectively controlled the fire, ensuring there were no reports of injuries. The initial outbreak took place at approximately 3:40 pm, and further details will be provided as the investigation unfolds.

title: Mumbai rains: Falling tree crushes school van in Chembur, 5 students hospitalised
content: The incident took place near Heritage Pride and was reported to the Mumbai Fire Brigade at 2.58 pm. Fire brigade personnel, police, a 108 ambulance and BMC ward staff rushed to the spot and carried out a rescue operation. Officials said all 15 students were travelling in the Universal High School van when the tree was uprooted and fell on the vehicle.

title: FIFA World Cup 2026 scripts unprecedented history after Germany, Netherlands crash out
content: A historic World Cup saw two top-10 nations, Germany and the Netherlands, crash out in the opening knockout round. Germany's campaign ended on penalties against Paraguay after a controversial disallowed goal. The Netherlands also fell to Morocco in a dramatic shootout, with Morocco's goalkeeper proving decisive. This marks a significant upset in the tournament's history.

title: R. Madhavan left stunned by 3-year-old drummer leading full orchestra
content: Actor R. Madhavan was left in awe by a viral video of Russian child prodigy Lyonya Shilovsky, who led a full orchestra at just three years old. The clip, showcasing the young drummer's exceptional rhythm and confidence, continues to inspire audiences worldwide. Shilovsky, who began drumming at one, became an internet sensation with his remarkable performance of "Can-Can," demonstrating a maturity rarely seen in such a young musician.

title: MDMK's Vaiko loses cool over DMK alliance question; supporters try to attack reporters
content: MDMK leader Vaiko lost his composure at a press meet when questioned about his criticism of the DMK. He alleged widespread corruption during the DMK's tenure, particularly in land registration. When pressed on why he hadn't raised these issues earlier, Vaiko cited coalition ethics. The situation escalated with his supporters attempting to confront reporters, but police intervention diffused the tension.

title: Cowboys linked to $53M Colts star in blockbuster NFL trade proposal
content: The Dallas Cowboys have been named a potential landing spot for Indianapolis Colts star Jonathan Taylor in a proposed trade. CBS Sports' Garrett Podell suggested the All-Pro running back could be available for a third-round pick if the Colts struggle in 2026. With Taylor entering the final year of his contract and coming off another standout season, Dallas could pursue him if its rushing attack fails to meet expectations.

title: Brittany Mahomes shares heartfelt promise inspired by Patrick Mahomes
content: Brittany Mahomes says becoming a mother has made her more ambitious and intentional about pursuing her goals. She hopes her children, Sterling, Bronze, and Golden, see both her and Patrick Mahomes working hard and following their dreams. Brittany believes leading by example will teach them that success comes through determination and perseverance. She also shared that her daughter Sterling has shown growing interest in her work, making those moments even more meaningful.

title: Ketan death case: Who is Siya's lawyer? Advocates' clash, Rs 10 cr notice adds twist
content: A legal battle has erupted over who represents murder accused Siya Goyal. Advocate Ashutosh Srivastava claims his appointment is valid and filed, threatening defamation suits against Siya's brother, Sahil, who denies engaging him. Another lawyer, Vipul Dushing, also claims representation. Siya and co-accused Chetan Chaudhary were remanded in police custody over the alleged murder of businessman Ketan Agarwal.

title: OFFSIDE | Japan and Germany are out — and why Sweden vs France is Westphalia with wingers
content: Day 19 saw Brazil survive Japan, Germany lose their penalty superpower, and Morocco send the Dutch into another philosophical crisis. Read this and more in today's Offside.

title: Ukraine strikes Russian satellite communications hub near Moscow again; Russia downs 419 drones
content: Ukraine has successfully targeted Russia's satellite communications center in Dubna for a second time, President Zelenskyy announced. This strike, over 500 kilometers from Ukraine's border, is part of Kyiv's strategy to cripple Russian military intelligence and coordination.  Similar facilities in the Moscow and Vladimir regions have also been hit, intensifying Ukraine's long-range drone campaign against key Russian infrastructure.

title: Europe heatwave: Indian AC makers eye market, face competition from China, South Korea
content: So far, exports have remained limited, partly because domestic air-conditioner penetration in India is still only around 10-11%. Air conditioners are also the only white goods segment covered under the production-linked incentive (PLI) scheme, prompting the government to push for higher export earnings from the industry.

title: Barrel of backups: India's crude imports bounce back despite Middle East disruptions
content: India's crude oil imports are nearing pre-conflict levels, thanks to refiners diversifying away from traditional Gulf suppliers.  Purchases from Russia, the US, and other regions have compensated for Middle East disruptions.  Russia remains a favored source due to attractive pricing.  While the Strait of Hormuz has reopened, Asian refiners are cautious about immediate increases in Gulf purchases, focusing on existing cargo coverage and maintenance schedules.

title: Old video of fake Army Brigadier Aryan Verma motivating UPSC aspirants resurfaces— watch
content: A resurfaced video shows Aryan Verma, arrested for impersonating an Indian Army Brigadier, addressing UPSC aspirants with armed guards. Verma, who claimed to have switched career ambitions from medicine to UPSC after a public event, encouraged interaction with outsiders. The 21-year-old was apprehended with associates after allegedly posing as a senior officer, travelling in a military-marked SUV and possessing fake credentials.

title: Khushbu Sundar gets emotional at daughter Avantika's wedding
content: Actress Khushbu Sundar became emotional at her elder daughter Avantika's intimate Goa wedding to Shravan Sreenivasan. A viral video captured her tears of joy. Following the ceremony, Khushbu shared heartfelt photos and defended her decision to post about the family milestone, even as the industry mourned a loss, asserting her right to celebrate personal happiness.

title: Tier-II cities to drive affordable housing finance growth through FY28: Report
content: Affordable housing finance companies are poised for strong growth, with assets expected to rise 19-20% annually through FY28. This surge is fueled by demand in smaller cities, improving housing affordability, and small business financing needs. While home loans will see steady expansion, loans against property are projected for faster growth, though lenders remain cautious on smaller ticket sizes due to past stress.

title: Japanese Yen hits four-decade low against US dollar - why the currency is depreciating
content: The last time the yen traded at these levels, it was moving in the opposite direction, strengthening during a prolonged rally that followed a currency agreement brokered by the United States.

title: 'Lured out for momos': Woman alleges sexual harassment inside moving car in Delhi
content: A 24-year-old woman has accused a man of sexual harassment and wrongful restraint in a moving car in East Delhi's Mandawali. The accused allegedly diverted the vehicle to a secluded area after promising a food stop, made obscene remarks, and touched her inappropriately. She managed to record a video, and police have registered an FIR and apprehended the suspect.

title: Shop Can’t Charge Round-Off Extra: Rajasthan panel rules; Jaipur mart fined ₹10,000
content: A Rajasthan consumer commission has ruled that shopkeepers cannot arbitrarily round off bills, even for a mere 50 paise. A Jaipur shop was found liable for deficiency in service and unfair trade practices after charging a customer extra for a rajma packet. The commission upheld the ruling, reducing the compensation to Rs 10,000 for the customer, emphasizing that such unauthorized charges are unacceptable.

title: Watch: Moroccan fans clash with Dutch police after Morocco stun Netherlands in WC
content: Morocco's stunning 3-2 penalty shootout victory over the Netherlands in the FIFA World Cup 2026 has ignited wild celebrations in Casablanca, with fans pouring into the streets. However, the joy was marred by clashes between police and fans in The Hague, where fireworks and stones were thrown. Captain Achraf Hakimi emphasised the win proves Morocco's Qatar success was no fluke, highlighting the team's dedication and hard work.

title: What is the Delhi Eye project? Everything to know about the capital's next landmark
content: East Delhi is set to welcome the "Delhi Eye," a towering observation wheel inspired by the London Eye. Planned by the DDA on 3.84 acres in Mayur Vihar, this 35-meter attraction aims to become a new landmark, offering panoramic city views. The project, which has faced delays, is being revived through private participation to boost tourism and recreational facilities in the capital.

title: 'Cocktail 2' BO Day 12: Shahid Kapoor starer eyes Rs 100 crore after Monday's 60% fall
content: Homi Adajania's 'Cocktail 2', starring Shahid Kapoor, Kriti Sanon and Rashmika Mandanna, hit a speed bump on Day 11, with collections dropping a steep 60.2% to Rs 1.75 crore as the second-weekend bump faded and Welcome to the Jungle continued commanding the majority of screens nationwide. As Day 12 live tracking shows the film adding Rs 0.22 crore so far across 1,360 shows, the domestic net stands at Rs 85.12 crore, with the total India gross crossing the symbolic Rs 100 crore mark and the worldwide gross at Rs 129.42 crore. The film's path to a domestic Rs 100 crore net milestone is just roughly Rs 15 crore away even as the worldwide gross collection stands at Rs. 129.42 crore.

title: How guidance kits are giving 'dumb' rockets the precision of a sniper rifle
content: Old 'dumb' rockets are getting a high-tech makeover, becoming precision weapons at a fraction of missile costs. This smart upgrade offers militaries affordable accuracy and greater battlefield flexibility. Countries like India, Europe, and Russia are embracing this trend, turning existing rocket arsenals into potent, cost-effective strike tools. This innovation is revolutionizing modern warfare economics.

title: ‘Golden gift to White House’: Trump shares AI image of giant eagle for 250th anniversary
content: US President Donald Trump shared an AI-generated image of a golden eagle on the White House balcony, calling it a "Golden Gift" for the nation's 250th anniversary. The White House reposted the image, sparking debate about its authenticity. The image, featuring architectural discrepancies and fewer stars on a shield, was confirmed to be AI-generated. This follows Trump's recent unveiling of a commemorative US passport design.

title: Nitish Reddy racing against time to be fit for England ODIs; Gill, Rahul to reach early
content: All-rounder Nitish Kumar Reddy faces a race against time for the upcoming ODI series against England, with a minor left quadricep pull sidelining him. While the management is optimistic about his return next month, his participation in England appears unlikely. The Zimbabwe T20Is are being considered as a potential comeback opportunity.

title: Congress delegation Ayodhya visit row: Yogi Adityanath slams party, says it is 'leaning on lies'
content: Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath sharply criticized the Congress over the Ram Temple donation row, accusing them of hypocrisy and past anti-devotee actions. He stated that those who once opposed Lord Ram are now eager to visit Ayodhya. This comes as the Congress alleged house arrests of its leaders, including state chief Ajay Rai, who planned to visit Ayodhya amid the embezzlement controversy.

title: Watch: Mexican fans swarm Ecuador team hotel with drums and chants before WC clash
content: Mexican football fans attempted to disrupt Ecuador's World Cup preparations by creating a noisy disturbance outside their team hotel in Mexico City.  Chanting and using horns throughout the night, supporters aimed to unsettle the visiting squad before their crucial Round of 32 match.  This incident precedes a high-stakes game where the winner advances to the Round of 16.

title: JDAMs to Chinese glide bombs: Weapons Pakistan used for airstrikes in Afghanistan
content: Pakistan has launched air strikes within Afghanistan, targeting the Tehreek-e-Taliban Pakistan following a series of attacks. Equipped with both imported and domestically produced precision-guided munitions, these operations aim at adversaries who do not possess air defenses. Unfortunately, recent strikes have also caused civilian casualties, including a devastating incident at a hospital, underscoring Pakistan's ongoing mission to elevate its aerial warfare capabilities.

title: Jio IPO: What is Project Jupiter? How Mukesh Ambani’s Reliance worked behind closed doors for India's biggest offering
content: Reliance engaged with regulators to secure greater flexibility in IPO rules, encouraging key investors to divest their holdings, and designing the country's largest public offering while keeping its structure under wraps. The highly confidential initiative was internally code-named Project Jupiter, reflecting both its scale and strategic importance.

title: Can’t afford a lawyer? Who can get free legal aid in India and how to apply
content: Accessing justice in India is now easier for those who cannot afford legal representation. The National Legal Services Authority (NALSA) offers free lawyers and legal assistance to specific vulnerable groups, including women, children, and individuals from Scheduled Castes/Tribes, irrespective of income. Others can qualify based on income thresholds, which vary by court level and state. Applications can be submitted in person or online, with decisions made swiftly.

title: Kerala govt. has asked private medical, dental colleges to abide by NMC directive on fees: Muraleedharan
content: NMC had directed that fee may be collected only for the course period of four-and-a-half years; Health Minister says decision was taken at a meeting that had been convened recently to restructure course fee

title: Jagannath Nayak takes over as DG Missiles & Strategic Systems (MSS) DRDO
content: He is the chief architect of Fibre Optic Gyro (FOG) guidance for Akash missiles and DEW-based Anti-Drone systems.

title: SIR in Karnataka: Door-to-door enumeration begins from Chief Minister’s home; D.K. Shivakumar urges voters to complete exercise
content: The Chief Minister said the SIR process had formally begun in the State and urged voters to ensure that their details in the electoral rolls were up to date

title: K.C. Venugopal moves privilege notice against Rajnath for ‘misleading’ Parliament about Operation Sindoor casualties
content: The Congress leader said that during the discussion held in July 2025 regarding the Pahalgam terror attack and subsequent Operation Sindoor, Rajnath Singh had told the House that no harm had come to Indian soldiers

title: SIR process lacks transparency: AAP
content: In the past, we have asked the Election Commission to provide us a list of names deleted from the list with address, but they have refused to do so, says AAP's Delhi unit chief Saurabh Bharadwaj

title: Monsoon arrived but rain didn’t, Telangana ends June with 12% rainfall deficit
content: Southwest monsoon, which runs from June to September, typically accounts for about 740.6 mm (80%) of Telangana’s annual rainfall of 923.8 mm

title: India, Pakistan should return to negotiating table: Mirwaiz
content: The Mirwaiz stated that India-Pakistan friendship is key to resolving ‘longstanding issues, including the Kashmir conflict’, and expressed hope that leaders from all sides will support peaceful efforts for resolution

title: Attempts to question FATF’s credibility often reflect fear of scrutiny: India at UN
content: India’s Permanent Representative to the UN Parvathaneni Harish said that countries facing adverse assessments should address the identified deficiencies, strengthen domestic enforcement, improve financial transparency, and demonstrate irreversible action against terror-financing networks

title: Renowned artist and activist K.T. Shivaprasad passes away at 78
content: Mr. Shivaprasad was widely known for his artistic practice, his work as an architect and photographer, and his steadfast support for the farmers' movement and the Dalit Sangharsha Samiti Karnataka.

title: Centre extends CBDT Chairman Ravi Agrawal’s tenure by six months
content: In a statement on Tuesday (June 30), the Appointments Committee of the Cabinet (ACC) said it has approved the “reappointment” of Mr. Agrawal as Chairman, CBDT on a contract basis for a period of six months

title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay to hold consultative meeting with post-poll alliance partners on July 1
content: The meeting convened by the TVK assumes significance against the backdrop of the disintegration of the DMK-led Secular Progressive Alliance following the 2026 Assembly poll debacle.

title: AIKS calls for nationwide farmers’ protests on August 10, opposes trade agreements
content: The meeting decided that AIKS would participate in the National Convention of the Samyukta Kisan Morcha (SKM) scheduled for July 28 in Delhi

title: CJP protest day 11: Dipke claims police blocking entry; Wangchuk's health deteriorates on fast
content: "Heavy police deployment at Jantar Mantar. The police are not allowing people to enter, and many of our core team members are being denied entry. What are they planning to do?" says Mr. Dipke

title: How similar are Uttarakhand, Gujarat and Assam’s Uniform Civil Codes?
content: As West Bengal is set to table a UCC bill, here is a comparison on the existing UCCs of Uttarakhand, Gujarat, and Assam

title: Opposition attacking EC over SIR to mask poll defeats, displaying ‘peak hypocrisy’: BJP
content: The charge came after 23 political parties and an Independent MP wrote to Chief Justice of India Surya Kant, raising concerns over the SIR process, the EC's functioning and other election-related issues

title: Ram temple donations embezzlement case: A timeline of events
content: Allegations of financial irregularities regarding donations to the Ram temple in Ayodhya gained pace in the past month, leading to the arrest of eight. Here is a timeline of key events.

title: Why Akal Takht is against Punjab’s anti-sacrilege law | Explained
content: The highest Sikh temporal seat has been annoyed over the Punjab government bringing in the amendments to the law without taking its opinion and consent and that of the SGPC and Sikh community

title: TET paper-leak scam: What it’s all about, how Maharashtra Police busted it | Explained
content: The Maharashtra TET paper leak case unpacks how the examination was allegedly compromised and why investigators suspect a wider interstate network

title: Kerala Assembly: Minister says ₹523-crore project approved to address Kochi drinking water shortage
content: Project awaiting final administrative sanction from Finance Department, says Mons Joseph in reply to a Calling Attention motion

title: SIR: No documents needed during house-to-house enumeration process, says Karnataka Chief Electoral Officer
content: Over 5.54 crore electors to be covered in month-long house-to-house drive beginning June 30; draft rolls on August 5

title: Bodies of teenaged inter-caste lovers found in Mayiladuthurai district; violence erupts
content: Police launch probe amid suspicion of foul play and caste killing; pickets posted at the village and bodies shifted to Tiruvarur Government Medical College Hospital for autopsy

title: AIADMK leader S.P. Velumani likely to resign as MLA: Party sources
content: Before departing for Chennai from Coimbatore airport on June 30, Mr. Velumani neither confirmed nor denied the likelihood of his resignation.

title: General Dhiraj Seth takes charge as new Army Chief
content: General Dhiraj Seth assumed the reins of the 1.3 million-strong force at a time when it is forging a path to become a self-reliant and future-ready Army while meeting challenges on the borders

title: Monsoon tracker LIVE: Monsoon intensifies across Maharashtra, Mumbai braces for heavy rain
content: The monsoon, which had shown a pause in its progress along the eastern coast, has gained momentum over the past 24 hours

title: Wars will be more integrated, theatre-oriented in future: General Dwivedi
content: General Upendra Dwivedi, an accomplished military leader with over 40 years of service who retires on June 30, was accorded a guard of honour as part of a ceremonial farewell held on the lawns of the South Block

title: U.S.-India relationship at its lowest in 30 years: Indian-American Democrat leader
content: Indian-American Congressman Ro Khanna claimed that during his recent visit to China, India’s Ambassador there had told him that a generation of trust was lost due to Mr. Trump’s policies

title: Complaint free implementation of welfare schemes and other challenges ahead for new Telangana CS Sanjay Jaju
content: Government’s priorities, demands from employees and central assistance for flagship schemes to pose tough time. The new CS will have to focus ensuring that senior officials visit the field.

title: Supreme Court refuses to entertain plea seeking CBI probe into ‘extra-judicial’ killing of Bharat Tiwari
content: A Bench headed by Justice M.M. Sundresh asked the petitioner, advocate Vishal Tiwari, to approach the Patna High Court for appropriate relief

title: Kerala Assembly: Minister says Transport dept mulling pre-licence training in schools for Plus Two students
content: C.P. John says the idea is to provide students licences by the time they finish Plus Two (when they turn 18) and that discussions will be held with General Education department

title: Kerala HC allows 3 crew members of MSC Elsa 3 to return to home countries on ₹1-lakh cash deposit
content: The three crew members allowed to travel back home have not been charged with any offences related to the ship capsizing in the final report submitted before a Judicial First Class Magistrate. HC had earlier imposed a bank guarantee condition

title: SIR enumeration begins at writer Banu Mushtaq’s residence in Hassan
content: Deputy Commissioner K.S. Latha Kumari and other officials of the administration, along with booth-level officers, visited Ms. Mushtaq’s residence and distributed enumeration forms

title: Sudha Kongara moves Madras HC to prevent release of ‘Idhayam Murali’ over ‘Parasakthi’ movie payment dispute
content: She claims that it is the only way to recover her pending dues of ₹8.39 crore for having directed actor Sivakarthikeyan-starrer ‘Parasakthi’

title: EC steps up digitisation of enumeration forms as SIR begins in Karnataka
content: To make the process more convenient, voters have been advised to use the ‘Book a Call with BLO’ feature available on the ECINet mobile application, which can be downloaded from the Google Play Store and Apple App Store

title: Kerala govt. plans to introduce marketing, business development division in Agriculture department: Minister Siddique
content: Agricluture Minister says directions have been given to map all government and privately owned barren land in the State; once mapping is done, next target is to achieve self-sufficiency in food production

title: Audio clip purportedly of Sadananda Gowda seeking Vijayendra to be ‘kicked out’ causes stir in BJP in Karnataka
content: The audio clip comes amidst speculations that the party high command may opt for a revamp of the party’s State leadership

title: As SIR begins on June 30, Karnataka govt. issues guidelines for permanent residence certificates
content: The applications will be processed by Atal Janasnehi Centres, BengaluruOne, KarnatakaOne and Grama One among others

title: Karnataka Sahitya Academy to chronicle lives of 59 freedom fighters jailed in Andaman and Nicobar Islands
content: The Academy has formed a committee comprising academicians and researchers, and they will be publishing a book on the lives of the freedom-fighters from Karnataka titled ‘Yaaroo Ariyada Veeraru’

title: Police Sub-Inspector in Bengaluru arrested in POCSO case involving minors
content: The officer is accused of taking the moinor boys, who were detained in connection with a murder investigation, to a lodge, assaulting them and forcing them into performing obscene acts, which he recorded on his mobile phone.

title: Anti-conversion law provides specific legal framework; innocent persons won't face trouble: Fadnavis
content: Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis said central approval is awaited for the implementation of the Act

title: TTD cancels Monday VIP Break, same-day Srivani darshan to prioritise common pilgrims
content: Privileged darshan quotas also curtailed as temple administration steps up crowd-management measures to cope with the unprecedented influx of pilgrims to Tirumala

title: Kerala Assembly passes resolution seeking reform of national entrance exam system, flags NEET concerns
content: In the resolution moved by Higher Education Minister, House cites serious concerns over credibility, transparency and fairness of NEET and other national-level exams

title: Patna court extends Khan Sir's protection from arrest till July 3 in coaching institute firing case
content: Faisal Khan was named in an FIR related to a firing incident by his guards when miscreants vandalised his coaching institute in early June

title: Monsoon intensifies across Maharashtra, Mumbai braces for heavy rain
content: IMD issues yellow alert for multiple districts; airlines caution passengers over possible flight disruptions in Mumbai and Dharamshala

title: Shiv Sena (UBT)’s Sachin Ahir joins Shinde faction, files Maharashtra Legislative Council Deputy Chairman nomination
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) leader Ambadas Danve confirmed that MLC Jagannath Abhyankar is the Maha Vikas Aghadi nominee

title: Congress leader's son detained, released over controversial hoardings featuring PM Modi, U.P. CM
content: The hoardings, installed at several locations between Bara Toll Plaza and the Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya in the Haidergarh area, have drawn widespread attention

title: What are India’s problems with most credit ratings agencies? | Explained
content: India has consistently been rated just a grade or two above ‘junk’ status, which is when institutions will stop lending money for fear of default

title: How a 60-year-old woman reclaims her memories through theatre
content: Veteran theatre artiste Sajitha Madathil and director Emil Madhavi discuss their one-woman play, Podcast Opera, that blends memory, ageing and the podcast format

title: Vasan urges Tamil Nadu government to fulfil doctors’ demands
content: In a call to action just ahead of National Doctors Day on July 1, Tamil Maanila Congress leader G.K. Vasan urged the Tamil Nadu government to address the longstanding demands of doctors, emphasizing their vital role in public health.

title: Embezzlement charges extremely serious and concerning, says Mayawati
content: Mayawati cautioned political parties that politicisation of religion is not right in the interest of the country and the people and this is also an appeal to the countrymen

title: Ketan Agarwal death case: What is gait analysis? | Explained
content: In clinical settings, gait analysis can be used to understand why someone is in pain or how a specific medical condition is progressing. Read on...

title: Delhi SIR: How to check your name in previous SIR and how to fill the enumeration form
content: As part of the process, electors must map their details with the electoral rolls prepared during Delhi’s previous revision in 2002

title: Opinion: Opinion | US' Scott Bessent Has A New 'Doctrine', And It Leaves India With Hard Choices
content: Scott Bessent in a recent WSJ opinion piece has explained the so-called American economic 'statecraft'. The doctrine should raise concerns in India.

title: Videos: Roads Melt, Tram Tracks Buckle As Europe Struggles With Heatwave
content: According to the World Health Organization (WHO), over 1,300 excess deaths had been recorded since 21 June "linked to high temperatures in Europe".

title: Stock Market Highlights, Sensex Today: Sensex Settles 249 Points Lower Amid Mixed Global Cues
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty Highlights: Brent August crude futures were down 1.03 per cent at $72.40 a barrel.

title: Delhi's EV Policy 2.0: What It Means For Legacy 2-Wheelers And Repair Shops
content: Delhi EV Policy 2.0: Major two-wheeler makers will have to compete with EV-focused companies that have been building electric since inception.

title: Ghaziabad Woman Kills Live-In Partner To "Protect" Teen Daughter, Tries To Pass It Off As Suicide
content: Two women, Kiran and her sister Kashish, killed Zakir inside their rented home in Tronica City on June 23.

title: 4 Killed, 27 Injured After Bus Rams Truck On Yamuna Expressway In UP
content: Senior Superintendent of Police Shlok Kumar said the accident took place around 4 am when the bus was going to Delhi from Lucknow.

title: Rare Dinosaur Fossil Found In Museum Drawer Decades After 1985 Discovery
content: A rare titanosaur tail bone, tucked in a drawer since 1985, has revealed dinosaurs once roamed Antarctica when it was lush and forested.

title: Man Kills Wife, Children, Then Dies By Suicide In Rajasthan Village
content: "The husband was found hanging while his wife and two children were lying on a cot. Prima facie, the husband strangled his wife and two children and then hanged himself," Superintendent of Police Satnam Singh said.

title: Lt General Dhiraj Seth Takes Charge As New Army Chief
content: The general officer succeeded General Upendra Dwivedi, who retired on Tuesday after an illustrious career spanning over 40 years in the armed forces.

title: Over 1,000 Drones Detected Near World Cup Stadiums, 300 Neutralized: FBI
content: The FBI detected 1,139 drones during the World Cup, neutralizing over 300 in a massive security operation that exceeded previous Olympic defense efforts.

title: Opinion: Opinion | China's New AI Surveillance System Has A Chilling New Mission
content: A new legislation in China, which takes effect tomorrow, isn't just another security law. It reaches across borders - and into the future.

title: Telegram-Like Risks? WhatsApp Username Move Draws Scam Warning
content: A lot of people on social media platform X are saying that this WhatsApp username feature can lead to impersonations and fraud.

title: Top Court Rejects Asaram's Bail Plea In Jodhpur Rape Case On Medical Grounds
content: The top court clarified that in case Asaram's health deteriorated, he can approach court for bail in the meanwhile.

title: BJP Chief Sets 2028 Telangana Target, Vows Double-Engine Government
content: Launching a sharp attack on the Congress government in the state, he alleged that it had failed to deliver on key promises, including employment.

title: "Will Take 2 Minutes": Man Sexually Harasses Woman In Delhi, She Films It
content: The woman alleged that the man made obscene remarks, demanded sexual favours, touched her inappropriately, and prevented her from leaving the car.

title: 3 Bangladeshi Nationals With Fake Ids Arrested In Kerala
content: Three Bangladeshi nationals arrested in Kerala for illegal stay and fake IDs during a migrant worker camp inspection.

title: Hours After US Deportation, Over 100 Venezuelans Trapped In Deadly Earthquake
content: Hours after a US deportation flight landed, a massive earthquake collapsed the processing hotel, leaving over 100 people missing in the rubble.

title: What To Know About Heat Domes As Global Temperatures Rise
content: As heat domes intensify worldwide, climate scientists explain what causes them, why they're worsening, and how to stay safe in extreme heat.

title: Births falling yet population rising: India's unique fertility story explained in 10 charts
content: the average Indian woman is now expected to have 1.9 children . in 2026, India is still set to add roughly 1.3 crore people through natural increase . decades of higher birth rates have built a huge cohort of young adults now entering their prime childbearing years .

title: We will hit back: New TNCC chief Tagore sets sight on PM Rahul Gandhi for 2029
content: manickam Tagore appointed the state president of the Tamil Nadu Congress Committee (TNCC) he warned that Congress would aggressively “hit back” against any criticism directed at the party or its leadership . he referred to former ally DMK and its leaders, labeling them “betrayers” and “backstabbers” for criticising Rahul Gandhi .
